sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check how many green blocks remain after these clicks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 5]
print(f"Green: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    center = (sum(r[0] for r in g['boundary'])//len(g['boundary']), sum(r[1] for r in g['boundary'])//len(g['boundary']))
    print(f"  center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Still 12 green blocks. Clicking the player at (38, 45) multiple times didn't convert any more blocks. It seems like clicking only converts one block per move. The pattern seems to be: move the player (LEFT/RIGHT), then click to convert the nearest green block. Let me try moving LEFT to get closer to more green blocks, then click.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move player LEFT to get closer to green blocks, then click
action([
    {'action': 'LEFT'},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 30}
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
